div.h2: Mutex Contention & Cache-Line False Sharing — server_stats_t
div.p: False sharing: Mutex write invalidates counter cache lines on all cores even though no counter was written.

g.text: MESI Protocol — Cache Coherence State Transitions

ul.li: Good layout: 128 B (Separated line 0 and line 1)

div.p: Every mutex acquire writes to cache line 0 → x86 LOCK CMPXCHG on lock field → CPU broadcasts RFO (Request For Ownership) on memory bus → All other cores' copies of line 0 → INVALID (I) → Cores reading counters must reload from L3/RAM → Even though NO counter was modified! Contested lock cost: ~100 ns (L3 miss) False sharing overhead: ~90 ns per acquisition

✓ Zero False Sharing → Mutex write dirties only cache line 0. → Cache line 1 (counters) stays in SHARED state. → No RFO broadcast for counter reads. Savings: ~86 ns per counter read during contention.
False Sharing: Byte 0x00 write invalidates entire 64B block. Threads reading byte 0x2C pay I→S reload cost.
Core 0 (Acquires Lock) LOCK CMPXCHG Line 0: M (Modified)
Core 1 (Reads Counter) Line 0: S→I Cost: ~100 ns stall
